compiled_state.title: Update project docs to reflect polyglot kudos trigger replacements
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update existing project documentation to reflect that T7, T9, T12, T13, and T4 are now polyglot (language-agnostic) triggers, that lizard and jscpd are required in the sprint acceptance environment, and that TRIGGER_SKIPPED is the expected behavior on stacks without the required tooling.

**Why**: After implementation, CLAUDE.md and any agent docs referencing the kudos trigger system will be stale — agents working on future kudos extensions or debugging trigger behavior need accurate documentation.

**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md (kudos system notes if any exist after implementation); KNOWN-ISSUES.md if any issues discovered during implementation need tracking; any agent definition or skill file that references Python-specific trigger behavior
- OUT: Creating new documentation files; documenting implementation details (implementation plan does that)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, any CLAUDE.md or agent doc section that described T7/T9/T12/T13/T4 as Python-specific now accurately reflects polyglot support
  ← Satisfies: documentation accuracy post-implementation
- When this story is complete, the sprint acceptance environment requirements (lizard, jscpd) are noted in the appropriate installation or configuration documentation

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for formatting, structure, and conventions
- [Scope] Update only existing files that are stale — do not create new docs

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for documentation formatting, structure, and conventions.
comments.author: Claude
